name: API-first and feasibility-first integration
preconditions: A workflow proposes an external service or platform integration
recommended_action: Verify official API availability, required permissions, operational constraints, and a smallest reversible feasibility check before implementation.
prohibited_actions: Treating browser automation or an unofficial endpoint as the default integration path
Promising delivery before feasibility is established
success_metrics: Feasibility decision records API availability, permissions, cost, and fallback
Implementation begins only after the smallest reversible check passes
failure_signals: Integration depends on an undocumented endpoint
Required access is discovered only after implementation starts

name: Credential isolation
preconditions: A workflow requires credentials, tokens, cookies, or account sessions
recommended_action: Keep credentials outside source, generated artifacts, logs, and shared handoffs; expose only the minimum scoped secret at runtime.
prohibited_actions: Writing plaintext credentials to repository files
Copying secrets into result JSON or diagnostic logs
Sharing one broad credential across unrelated services
success_metrics: No plaintext secret appears in tracked or generated content
Each integration uses a minimum-scope credential boundary
failure_signals: A secret is recoverable from a repository or artifact

name: Human approval gate
preconditions: An action publishes, spends money, changes external state, or creates material legal or reputational risk
recommended_action: Pause before the consequential action and require an identified human approver to review the evidence, scope, and rollback path.
prohibited_actions: Inferring approval from silence
Reusing approval for a materially changed scope
success_metrics: Approval identity, time, and approved scope are recorded
No gated action occurs before approval
failure_signals: External state changes without an approval record
Approval evidence does not match the executed scope

name: License gate
preconditions: A dependency, plugin, model, dataset, template, media asset, or third-party code will be adopted or redistributed
recommended_action: Verify license identity, permitted use, attribution, redistribution, and commercial-use conditions before adoption.
prohibited_actions: Assuming public availability means reusable
Shipping an asset with an unknown or incompatible license
success_metrics: License and required obligations are recorded before use
Distribution matches the verified license conditions
failure_signals: Required attribution or redistribution terms are unmet

name: Evidence and rights fail closed
preconditions: Content or a decision depends on factual evidence, ownership, consent, or usage rights
recommended_action: Block the unsupported claim or asset when evidence or rights cannot be verified, and surface the missing requirement explicitly.
prohibited_actions: Filling an evidence gap with an invented citation
Publishing while rights status is unknown
Silently downgrading a required gate to a warning
success_metrics: Every released claim and asset has traceable evidence or rights status
Missing evidence prevents release and produces a clear diagnostic
failure_signals: Released content contains an untraceable claim
An asset is used with unknown rights

name: One writer per file
preconditions: Multiple workers operate concurrently in one repository
recommended_action: Assign exactly one active writer to each file and reserve shared status documents and integration changes for the integration owner.
prohibited_actions: Assigning overlapping file ownership to parallel workers
Letting workers edit shared status documents without explicit ownership
success_metrics: Every work order names owned and prohibited files
Parallel handoffs merge without overlapping edits
failure_signals: Two workers modify the same file concurrently
A shared document contains conflicting updates

name: Domain lead release flow
preconditions: A change spans specialized domain logic, integration, QA, and release decisions
recommended_action: Have the domain lead validate domain contracts, then hand off to the integration owner for cross-lane review, QA evidence, documentation, and final release action.
prohibited_actions: Allowing a domain lane to self-release without integration review
Treating implementation completion as release readiness
success_metrics: Domain acceptance and integration acceptance are separately recorded
Release includes QA evidence and a clear rollback state
failure_signals: A change releases without domain-owner acceptance
Integration defects appear after an isolated lane passes

name: Skill scripts resources and examples structure
preconditions: A reusable skill needs executable helpers, reference material, or usage demonstrations
recommended_action: Keep deterministic automation in scripts, selectively loaded knowledge and templates in resources, and concise representative usage in examples.
prohibited_actions: Embedding large reference material directly in the main skill instruction
Duplicating deterministic helper logic as prose
success_metrics: Skill instructions route to the smallest relevant supporting file
Scripts and examples are independently understandable and reusable
failure_signals: The main instruction file grows with unrelated reference content
Users must reconstruct a helper that should be executable

name: Skill golden cases
preconditions: A skill has stable expected behavior that can be demonstrated and regression checked
recommended_action: Maintain representative golden inputs and expected outputs or acceptance properties, including critical failure and gate behavior.
prohibited_actions: Treating a prose description as sufficient regression coverage
Updating golden expectations only to make a broken result pass
success_metrics: Golden cases cover the primary success path and protected failure gates
Behavior changes produce an explicit golden-case review
failure_signals: A regression passes because no representative case exists
